mitre: T1547.001 - Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
description: Programs listed here launch automatically for all users at logon.
security_note: The number one persistence location responders check. Any entry pointing to %TEMP%, %APPDATA% or an unsigned binary is suspicious.

mitre: T1547.001 - Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
description: Per-user autostart entries that launch when this user logs on.
security_note: Writable without admin rights, so it is the favourite persistence spot for low-privilege malware.

mitre: T1547.001 - Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
description: Entries here run once at the next logon and are then deleted.
security_note: Used by legitimate installers, but also by malware that wants a single stealthy execution after reboot.

description: Controls the shell and the userinit process launched by winlogon.exe at logon.
security_note: Appending a second path to Shell or Userinit is stealthy persistence executed by winlogon.exe under the user.

mitre: T1546.012 - Image File Execution Options Injection
description: Per-executable launch options. A 'Debugger' value under a program's subkey hijacks that program's execution.
security_note: Classic accessibility-tool hijack (sethc.exe) and a way to blind EDR by attaching a fake debugger. Watch for new Debugger values.

mitre: T1543.003 - Create or Modify System Process: Windows Service
description: Every Windows service and driver is configured under this key.
security_note: A new service subkey with a suspicious ImagePath is service persistence, often running as SYSTEM.

description: Configuration for the Print Spooler service.
security_note: Start=0x2 means Automatic; an ImagePath pointing anywhere other than System32 indicates a hijacked or masqueraded service.

description: Local Security Authority configuration, including LSASS protection settings.
security_note: RunAsPPL=1 protects lsass from credential dumping. Attackers disable it or abuse Notification/Authentication Packages for credential theft.

mitre: T1548.002 - Abuse Elevation Control: Bypass UAC
description: User Account Control and system policy settings.
security_note: EnableLUA=0 turns UAC off entirely; attackers flip this or abuse auto-elevating binaries to bypass prompts.

mitre: T1003.002 - OS Credential Dumping: Security Account Manager
description: The SAM database branch storing local account password hashes.
security_note: Combined with the SYSTEM hive, offline SAM yields NTLM hashes. Its ACL restricts access to SYSTEM; 'reg save HKLM\SAM' by a user is a red flag.

description: Runs a StubPath command once per user at their first interactive logon.
security_note: A lesser-known persistence spot; StubPath values execute in the user context and are easy to overlook.

description: Defines which services and drivers load in Safe Mode (Minimal and Network).
security_note: Some ransomware registers itself here so it still runs after rebooting into Safe Mode, where AV may be inactive.

mitre: T1037.001 - Boot or Logon Initialization Scripts: Logon Script (Windows)
security_note: A UserInitMprLogonScript value here runs at logon - stealthy, user-level persistence that needs no admin rights.

description: Inventory of installed programs (used by Add/Remove Programs).
security_note: Not a persistence location, but responders read it for a software inventory during triage.
